draft.introduction: Proverbs 31:10–31 is the acrostic poem of the “woman of valor” (eshet chayil), celebrating a woman of strength, industry, and wisdom. It closes the entire book of Proverbs as a portrait of embodied wisdom.

translation.text: An excellent wife who can find? She is far more precious than jewels. The heart of her husband trusts in her, and he will have no lack of gain. Strength and dignity are her clothing, and she laughs at the time to come. She opens her mouth with wisdom. Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain, but a woman who fears the Lord is to be praised.
translation.original_text: γυναῖκα ἀνδρείαν τίς εὑρήσει τιμιωτέρα δέ ἐστιν λίθων πολυτελῶν

translator_notes.note: The Hebrew eshet chayil is better rendered “woman of valor” or “woman of strength” than “virtuous wife.” The same word chayil describes warriors and armies elsewhere. The Septuagint’s γυναῖκα ἀνδρείαν (“courageous woman”) captures this martial connotation.
